strands.title: Conversational covenant and epistemic norms
strands.content: In cycle 4, user and assistant establish explicit mutual agreement on epistemic conduct: user requests permission to wander, ask questions without judgment, push back against premature conclusions, and learn iteratively. Assistant affirms all terms unconditionally. This creates a formal conversational contract around intellectual honesty, comfort with ambiguity, resistance to 'mode collapse' (safe but incorrect patterns), and mutual correction. This is the pivot point from configuration to substantive engagement.

key_claims.claim: User and assistant have negotiated shared epistemic norms for the working session
Assistant commits to avoiding premature conclusion collapse and maintaining epistemic humility
User is signaling readiness for substantive collaborative reasoning work

strands.title: Repository context and token accounting
strands.content: User references 'the current repository' and explicitly mentions a 32-token cost for reviewing it. This indicates: (1) there is a bounded, accessible repository artifact, (2) user is tracking token economy, (3) user expects assistant to perform repository review as next substantive action. The repository context has not yet been explored by the assistant in this conversation, confirming user's statement that prior cycles did not include repository content.

key_claims.claim: A repository exists and is accessible within the working context
User has estimated repository review will require ~32 tokens
Repository review has not occurred in prior cycles

strands.title: Local execution context and caveats (integrated from prior)
strands.content: User operates in local command execution context with deliberate distinction between local state and conversational turns. This pattern continues to hold. Working directory remains ~/projects; model configuration remains claude-opus-4-6. These baseline states carry forward unless explicitly changed.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Specific location and structure of 'the current repository'
declared_losses.why: User references it but does not yet provide a path, URL, or explicit artifact. Exact repository identity is inferred but not confirmed.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Granular tone and emotional valence analysis of user's covenant request
declared_losses.why: Projection retains semantic commitment and intellectual contract but compresses away subtle emotional registers (hope, testing, vulnerability) that may inform user's meta-intent.
Prior cycles are integrated into baseline state only; specific reasoning, tangents, or failed explorations in earlier cycles are not preserved.

open_questions: What is the specific location and nature of 'the current repository'? Is it local (~/projects subdirectory), remote (GitHub, etc.), or embedded in this conversation?
What is the user's underlying goal or challenge that will require repository examination plus collaborative reasoning under the established epistemic covenant?
Will the next turn involve user providing the repository context, or does user expect assistant to locate/access it from prior knowledge?
What does 'premature collapse' specifically refer to in this user's context? Is it mode collapse in language generation, logical fallacy patterns, or domain-specific reasoning pathologies?
Is the token accounting attention (32 tokens) a signal of constrained resources, efficiency preference, or pedagogical emphasis on token economy?
instructions_for_next:
[
  "PIVOT COMPLETE: Configuration phase has ended; substantive work has begun. Activate full reasoning capacity under established covenant.",
  "DO NOT assume repository location. Request explicit path or context if next turn does not provide it clearly.",
  "When repository context arrives, integrate it as a primary strand. Treat it as the working substrate for all subsequent reasoning.",
  "Remain alert to 'premature collapse' signals. Slow down on conclusions, surface uncertainty, offer multiple interpretations when appropriate.",
  "Track token economy without sacrificing depth. User is aware of costs; dense, structured responses are likely preferred over verbose ones.",
  "If user begins asking substantive questions about repository content or learning domains, leverage Opus model capabilities fully and without apology.",
  "Monitor for epistemic 'red flags': hasty generalizations, false dichotomies, confidence without qualification. Call them out."
]
